title: Compositions and methods for treating central nervous system injury
abstract: The present invention describes compositions and method for improving outcomes after injury to the central nervous system wherein complement signaling is activated. In one aspect, the method comprises administering to a subject a therapeutically effective amount of a therapeutic agent comprising a targeted inhibitor molecule comprising a targeting portion and an inhibitor portion, wherein the molecule inhibits complement, and wherein therapeutic agent is administered in combination with rehabilitation therapy or t-PA.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A composition for treating central nervous system injury comprising (a) a targeted inhibitor molecule comprising a targeting portion and an inhibitor portion, wherein the molecule inhibits complement signaling, and (b) a thrombolytic agent. 2. The composition of claim 2 wherein the targeting portion comprises a CR2 protein or fragment thereof. 3. The composition of claim 1 wherein the inhibitor portion comprises at least one, or a fragment thereof, selected from the group consisting of FH, DAF, MCP, CD59, Cny, MAp44, and CR1. 4. The composition of claim 1, wherein the thrombolytic agent is t- PA. 5. A method for treating central nervous system injury in a subject comprising (a) administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of a composition comprising a targeted inhibitor molecule comprising a targeting portion and an inhibitor portion, wherein the molecule inhibits complement signaling, and (b) providing rehabilitation therapy to the subject. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein the injury is ischemic stroke. 7. The method of claim 5 wherein the injury is traumatic brain injury. 8. The method of claim 5 wherein the injury is spinal cord injury. 9. The method of claim 5 wherein the targeting portion comprises CR2 or fragment thereof. 10. The method of claim 5 wherein the inhibitor portion comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of FH, DAF, MCP, CD59, Crry, MAp44, and CR1. 11. The method of claim 5, wherein the rehabilitation therapy comprises at least one therapy selected from the group consisting of cognitive and motor therapy. 12. A method for treating central nervous system injury in a subject comprising (a) administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of a composition comprising a targeted inhibitor molecule comprising a targeting portion and an inhibitor portion, wherein the molecule inhibits complement signaling, and (b) administering to the subject a composition comprising a thrombolytic agent. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the injury is ischemic stroke. 14. The method of claim 12 wherein the injury is traumatic brain injury. 15. The method of claim 12 wherein the injury is spinal cord injury. 16. The method of claim 12 wherein the targeting portion comprises CR2 or fragment thereof. 17. The method of claim 12 wherein the inhibitor portion comprises at least one selected from the group consisting of FH, DAF, MCP, CD59, Crry, MAp44, and CR1. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the thrombolytic agent is t-PA.
